topic: Redundancy check — sections saying the same thing? Anything that can be tightened?
change_summary: Phase 2 operator-commissioned rerun of HOL-REDUNDANCY (c1: 2026-04-06). ~7 weeks of substantive content changes since c1 — 72 WINs, 14 top-level sections (part8b added since c1), sections.json grew to 704 KB. Re-evaluating whether the c1 redundancy clusters have been consolidated, whether new clusters have emerged, and whether c1 stronger-arguments recommendations were adopted.
overall_assessment: The Phase 2 rerun finds the c1 diagnosis substantially intact and in places worse. Of the five major redundancy clusters c1 flagged, two have been partially consolidated (95.2% hardcoded; relabeling-standard-physics is now anchored in part2b but still echoed in part6), one has held steady (Sydney-Perth, redistributed but still 4 locations), and two have grown worse (eclipse/Chapman now spread across 7 sections including the new part8b, with part4 alone carrying 144 eclipse-related mentions; aetheric refraction in Section 1.5 and Section 7.11 still both deliver near-complete treatments). The c1 stronger_arguments recommendation to add an upfront reading guide remains unimplemented — part1 opens with the h2 'How This Review Is Organized' but the content underneath does not explain why themes recur across sections. Two NEW redundancy clusters have emerged since c1: (i) OTS/timestamp-prediction-document argumentation now appears in part4 (25), part6 (36), part8 (25), and part8b (11), with no single canonical home despite CLAUDE.md asserting 'the dedicated write-up lives in the Timestamp Error tab' — no such dedicated section exists; the canary write-up is scattered; (ii) the 'ensemble anchor / W004 magnitude-only Pass band / Chapman ⊇ dome' argument from the eclipse expansion work now appears in part2b (8), part4 (8), part6 (10), part8 (3), part8b (18), and part9 (5) — 52 mentions across 6 sections, with part8b alone re-deriving the four-layer eclipse stack that part4 §4.2 already presents. Net assessment: the document is structurally LESS consolidated than at c1, primarily because each major expansion (part8b, the eclipse-layered analysis, the OTS canary surfacing) added fresh prose to multiple sections rather than designating canonical hubs with spoke summaries elsewhere. The c1 verdict and recommended_action both still hold; severity is reaffirmed at 'major' for eclipse and aetheric-refraction clusters; ratings are adjusted upward for the new OTS and ensemble-anchor clusters.
our_argument_summary: c1 (2026-04-06) found 8 redundancy clusters (2 major, 4 moderate, 2 minor) totaling ~4,000–6,000 recoverable words. Recommended hub-and-spoke architecture — designate canonical sections, reduce other locations to 1–2-sentence summaries with cross-references. Recommended adding an upfront reading guide explaining the intentional structural layering. Recommended action: minor_edit.

holes_found.description: Eclipse / Chapman cluster has gotten WORSE since c1. c1 flagged 6 locations (WIN-010, WIN-025, Section 3.2, Kill-Shot Test 5, Section 3.5.7, Section 6.2, version-history note) recommending Section 3.2 (now Section 4.2) as the canonical hub. Status as of c2: Section 4.2 ('The August 2026 Eclipse: A Sophisticated But Non-Discriminating Prediction') now spans 4.2.1–4.2.7a and is much more elaborated than at c1, which is good — it IS the canonical hub. But eclipse/Chapman content is now present in part1 (55 eclipse mentions), part4 (144 mentions, mostly the 4.2 hub), part5 Kill-Shot Test 5 (20), part6 (85, including 6.7 'The Eclipse Prediction: Genuine Test or Pre-Fitted?', 6.11–6.14 prospective bucket / κ vise), part8 §8.11 (13), and the NEW part8b §8b.3 four-layer eclipse stack (39). The Chapman 1933 / 5–25 nT range is re-stated by name in part4 (21 sentences), part5 (6), part6 (3), part8 (2), and part8b (7) — 39 separate re-derivations in five sections. The four-layer L1/L2/L3a/L3b structure adopted at part8b.3 substantially re-derives the same argument structure part4 §4.2.4 ('The four E-PRED tests') presents. This is exactly the hub-and-spoke failure c1 warned about, with the part8b addition making it worse.
holes_found.recommendation: Restate the canonical-hub commitment: Section 4.2 is the eclipse hub. Sections that currently re-derive the Chapman comparison should reduce to a one-sentence summary plus a cross-reference. Most urgent: §8b.3 should NOT re-present the four-layer stack — it should reference §4.2.4 and add only the fair-auditor-list-specific framing ('under adoption we restructure into the four layers shown at §4.2.4'). The §6.7 / §6.14 prospective-bucket and κ-vise analyses should retain only the new argumentative content (cross-event coupling, prospective-vs-retrodictive sorting) and reference §4.2 for the Chapman comparison. Estimated recoverable words: ~3,000.

holes_found.description: Aetheric refraction cluster has NOT consolidated. c1 flagged Section 1.5 (mechanism) and Section 4.9 (now §7.11 'Aetheric Refraction: The Universal Escape Hatch') as the two full treatments. Status as of c2: §1.5 has 21 aetheric-refraction sentences re-deriving the formula n(r) = 1 + 0.20·(8537/H(r)−1), discussing the fitted coefficient, and arguing unfalsifiability. §7.11 has 17 sentences doing substantially the same work in a different rhetorical frame ('falsification test' vs 'mechanism description'). Both sections re-derive the formula. Both argue unfalsifiability. Both compute that n reaches ~2.84 at the ice wall. Both note the 0.20 coefficient is fitted. The c1 recommendation — merge mechanism into §1.5, reduce §7.11 to a focused falsification framing referencing §1.5 — was NOT applied. A reader who works through linearly still encounters what feels like the same essay twice. §7.11 has ONE cross-reference back to §1.5 ('see Section 7.2.') but it occurs in a sentence about a different topic, not as the consolidation marker. Auxiliary uses (part2 §2.4 / §2.9 invocations, part5 Sydney-Perth refraction defense, part8 §8.5 Rule 11 mention) are appropriately brief.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the c1 recommendation as a concrete patch: cut §7.11 to ~6 paragraphs that (a) state the falsification test ('given the mechanism described in §1.5, here is the test that would falsify it'), (b) execute the test (no n(r) signature in atmospheric refraction data, lensing data, GPS timing data), (c) note that any failure is absorbable by re-tuning the 0.20 coefficient — that is the unfalsifiability proof. The current §7.11 explains what aetheric refraction IS for 9 paragraphs before getting to the falsification framing — that intro should be a single sentence pointing at §1.5. Estimated recoverable words: ~1,200.

holes_found.description: NEW redundancy cluster (post-c1): OTS / timestamp-prediction-document argumentation has no canonical hub despite CLAUDE.md asserting 'the dedicated write-up lives in the Timestamp Error tab'. No section in sections.json has 'timestamp' in its title. The closest dedicated home is part8 §8.5 'Directive #4: Dome Predictions Are Prospective and Timestamped' — but that is framed as one of eleven AI-context directives and not as the canonical timestamp-methodology section. Meanwhile timestamp-related content (OTS, status_history, prediction document, prospective claim) appears in part4 (25 mentions, including registry framing audit §4.6 and the eclipse hedging §4.2.3), part6 (36 mentions, mostly the prospective-bucket §6.11 and the steelman evidence ladder §6.10), part8 (25 mentions, §8.5 + scattered references), part8b (11 mentions, the fair-auditor-list response). The total is 109 mentions across 6 sections. The argument structure — 'OTS anchors status_history.json, not the prediction; prediction lives in monitor.py + model.html which are git-mutable; therefore timestamps establish content integrity not temporal priority' — is re-derived in §6.11 and §8.5 and partially in §4.6. A defender pasting our review into an AI sees the SAME argument three times in three frames and can plausibly claim we are padding our case.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) designate §8.5 as the canonical hub and reduce other locations to one-liners with cross-references, OR (b) create a dedicated '8c' or '1c' section titled 'The OpenTimestamps Methodology — What Is Anchored, What Is Not' and migrate the canonical argument there. Option (b) is preferable because §8.5 is currently locked into the directive-list framing of part8, which obscures the methodology argument. The dome-model-review CLAUDE.md already presupposes a 'Timestamp Error tab' exists — promoting the content to a dedicated section would match the project's stated architecture and answer the operator-side canary discipline explicitly. Estimated recoverable words: ~2,000.

holes_found.description: NEW redundancy cluster (post-c1): the ensemble-anchor / W004 magnitude-only Pass band / Chapman-band-arithmetic argument is restated in part2b (8 mentions, §2b.6 'April 2026 Scoreboard Drift'), part4 (8, §4.1 28-Scored/39-Domain dashboard), part6 (10, §6.14 'The κ Coupling Cluster — A Cross-Eclipse Vise'), part8 (3, §8.16 Monitoring Infrastructure), part9 (5, §9.2 'The Eclipse Test: Not What It Appears'), and the new part8b (18, §8b.3 four-layer eclipse stack). The argument — '(baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared arithmetic between dome W004 recalibration and standard Chapman-Sq-dynamo; therefore the dome cannot be discriminated from the globe in the magnitude band' — is fundamental and persuasive, but it appears 52 times across 6 sections with no single canonical statement. §8b.3 alone uses 'magnitude-only' 4 times in adjacent sentences. A defender skimming would reasonably argue we have one good point dressed up as six.
holes_found.recommendation: Pick a canonical hub for this argument. The natural home is §2b.6 (April 2026 Scoreboard Drift) because the argument is structurally about the dome's repository-encoded ensemble fit, which is part2b's domain. State the full arithmetic argument there once, and reduce all other instances to one-sentence references ('the magnitude-only Pass band cannot discriminate dome from globe — see §2b.6 for the arithmetic'). §8b.3's L1/L2/L3a/L3b layered stack should still LIST the layers but reference §2b.6 for the discrimination argument rather than re-deriving it. Estimated recoverable words: ~1,500.

holes_found.description: Sydney-Perth has held roughly steady but redistributed. c1 flagged 4 locations. c2 finds it in part2 §2.9 (8 mentions, the canonical V13 coordinate-system loop analysis — good), part5 (20 mentions, dominated by §5.0 'April 17 2026 Update — Author-Initiated Kill-Shot Demotions' which is genuinely new content responding to the dome's own demotion action), part6 (6 mentions, §6.2 disc-diameter provenance and STEELMAN-01), part7 §7.1 (5 mentions, Southern Hemisphere Distances — focuses on Sydney–Buenos Aires out-of-sample failure, complements the in-sample fit). The 4-location count is unchanged from c1. Part5's growth is justified — it covers the post-c1 dome author action. Part2 §2.9 is correctly the canonical hub. But part6 STEELMAN-01 and part7 §7.1 still re-state the 4,352/3,893/460 km numbers rather than referencing §2.9.
holes_found.recommendation: Section 2.9 is the canonical hub (confirmed). Part6 STEELMAN-01 and part7 §7.1 should drop the explicit 4,352/3,893/460 numbers and replace with '(see §2.9 for the internal-disagreement arithmetic)'. Part5 §5.0 is fine to keep verbose — it addresses a different question (the author's own demotion and its implications). Estimated recoverable words: ~400.

holes_found.description: Reading-guide recommendation from c1 stronger_arguments was NOT adopted. c1 recommended 'a brief reading guide paragraph in the Overview explaining the intentional structure: Part 1 describes the model, Part 2 evaluates each claim, ...' to pre-empt the 'repetitive' criticism. Current state: part1 contains an h2 titled 'How This Review Is Organized' but the content under that h2 (verified by inspection) does not explain the layering — it does not say 'the same arguments recur across tabs because each tab views the model through a different analytical lens.' This recommendation is independent of the consolidation work above and would help a tab-skipping reader (and an AI auditor) understand why redundancy is partially deliberate.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a 2–3 sentence paragraph under the 'How This Review Is Organized' h2 explaining: (a) the document evaluates the dome from multiple analytical angles (observation, geometry, code, AI directives, predictions, falsification tests, conclusions); (b) some arguments — especially eclipse non-discrimination, aetheric-refraction unfalsifiability, the Sydney–Perth internal disagreement, the relabeling pattern, and the OTS wrong-side fix — recur across tabs because they apply through each lens; (c) the canonical hub for each recurring argument is named (with anchor links). The third sentence does double duty: explains the redundancy AND creates the cross-reference scaffolding the consolidations above need.

holes_found.description: Relabeling-standard-physics has partially consolidated but still echoes. c1 recommended §4.6.2 (now §2b.2 'Relabeling Standard Physics') as the canonical home with §3.5.4 (now §6.4 'Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions') reduced to a summary. Status as of c2: §2b.2 IS the canonical hub (37 mentions in part2b, backed by code_analysis tag data). But §6.4 still exists as a major h2 in part6 with substantial standalone treatment (25 mentions in part6, mostly clustered around this section). The two sections present the same argument from two angles — predictions-page-claims (§6.4) and code-analysis-tag-data (§2b.2) — without §6.4 having been reduced to a summary as c1 recommended.
holes_found.recommendation: Reduce §6.4 to ~2 paragraphs that (a) state the relabeling pattern as it appears on predictions.html specifically (with 2–3 concrete examples — Schumann, Polaris, eclipse magnetic), and (b) reference §2b.2 for the comprehensive analysis backed by the code_analysis tags. Keep the predictions-page-specific framing because it is the natural fit for part6; cut the general argumentation that duplicates §2b.2. Estimated recoverable words: ~600.

holes_found.description: The '0 nT strawman' argument (dome publishes 'globe predicts 0.0 nT' as its falsification criterion when the true globe prediction is 5–25 nT via Chapman) is restated in part4 (10), part5 (7), part6 (18!), part8 (11), part8b (3) — 49 mentions, no canonical home. This is part of the eclipse cluster but worth flagging separately because it is a smaller atomic argument that nonetheless gets re-derived independently in many places. Part 6 alone has 18 mentions because the prospective-bucket analysis (§6.11) and the κ-vise (§6.14) each re-state the strawman.
holes_found.recommendation: Designate §4.2.3 'The hedging structure' as the canonical statement of the 0 nT strawman (it is the natural fit because the hedging analysis is what makes the strawman visible). Reduce other instances to '(see §4.2.3 for the hedging-structure analysis)'. Estimated recoverable words: ~800.

holes_found.description: Tidal WINs (WIN-045/046/049/050/051) consolidation from c1 has been substantially applied — each entry now has WIN-specific content (M2 = lunar circuit division, S2 = solar circuit, K1 = luni-solar diurnal, O1 = lunar declination, N2 = elliptical-orbit eccentricity). detail_evidence lengths are 1209–2622 chars and the bodies are no longer near-identical. Group field is preserved (WIN-045/046/049/050/051). This c1 finding can be considered RESOLVED. Minor remaining issue: the per-WIN entries still each re-derive the moon-at-2,534-km / one-tidal-spike-not-two-bulges geometric argument in the first paragraph; this geometric argument could move to a single shared introduction (the analyst already has the detail_group field for this purpose) leaving each entry focused on its unique constituent.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish: use detail_group's intended pattern — single shared geometric intro at the group level, per-WIN content limited to constituent-specific physics. Low priority since the c2 entries already differentiate well. Estimated recoverable words: ~500 (low impact).

holes_found.description: 'Zero fitted parameters' (§6.1 canonical) and tracking-page-inconsistency (Pattern 9) carry-overs from c1: both have remained roughly at c1's redundancy footprint. Tracking inconsistency has 23 mentions across 5 sections (part1b 6, part4 8, part8 4, part6 3, part9 2). §6.1 is the canonical statement and other mentions are brief — this c1 finding remains at the same minor severity it had at c1.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action required beyond c1's recommendation, which was minor. Optionally add the §6.1 cross-reference in §2b.3 ('Post-Hoc Retrodiction') as c1 recommended — quick edit if a decider patch round visits part2b.

stronger_arguments.description: The Reading Guide recommendation from c1 has become MORE important because part8b is a new section that itself reads as a partial reading guide (it explicitly catalogs what changes under each fair-auditor-list point). If the document had an authoritative reading guide in part1, part8b could anchor to it rather than acting as a parallel hub. Adding the reading guide also creates the cross-reference scaffolding the consolidations need — without anchor IDs and a named hub for each cluster, the spokes have nowhere to point.

A counter-intuitive consequence of the part8b addition: the document is now structurally stronger because each major rebuttal cluster has a named adoption status ('adopted', 'adopted partially', 'rejected'), but the per-cluster argument bodies are duplicated in their original section AND in their part8b treatment. Consolidating the bodies into one canonical location (per cluster) and using part8b solely for the adoption-status framing would tighten the document by ~1,500 words AND make part8b's status framing sharper.
sources: Direct inspection of part8b vs part4/part6 structural overlap

stronger_arguments.description: The dome-author-contact prior (CLAUDE.md: 'Update your priors: a dome-site update within 24-72h of a substantive change to our review, on the same WIN/section we updated, should be classified likely-responsive') gives consolidation a NEW motivation beyond word count: if we consolidate a single canonical statement of (e.g.) the OTS wrong-side argument and the dome author then patches OTS to anchor a prediction-side document, that is a clean canary fire on a specific anchor URL. Distributed redundancy weakens the canary because a fix might respond to any of five paraphrases, making the response signal harder to attribute. Consolidating sharpens the canary.

kernel_of_truth.description: Some of the post-c1 redundancy growth is genuinely valuable. Part8b is a new section answering a different question (which fair-auditor-list points materially change our text) and it had to recap the points it was adjudicating — that is responsive structure, not bloat. The eclipse-layered analysis added since c1 is genuinely new analytical content (the L1/L2/L3a/L3b decomposition was not present at c1). The OTS argumentation has grown because the author-contact event and the canary discipline created new contexts where the methodology fix matters. Pure subtraction would lose information.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The consolidations recommended above preserve the analytical content — they replace re-derivation with cross-references. The L1/L2/L3a/L3b layer structure can be presented ONCE (in §4.2.4 where it naturally lives) and referenced from §8b.3 without losing the fair-auditor-list framing. The OTS methodology argument can live in a dedicated section and be referenced from §6.11 prospective-bucket and §4.6 registry-framing-audit without losing those sections' specific analytical angles. The redundancy critique is about re-derivation, not about mention. Brief mention + cross-reference satisfies both the tab-skimming reader and the integrity-conscious auditor.

cross_win_consistency.notes: Cross-WIN consistency is not the focus of this holistic review (HOL-CROSSREF handles that), but a quick scan confirms the eclipse WIN cluster (WIN-010, WIN-011, WIN-068) reads consistently across the various sections that touch it — the Chapman 5–25 nT range is the same number every time, the W004 ensemble is described the same way. The risk c1 warned about (cross-reference drift when one instance is updated independently) has not yet materialized as a concrete drift, but the larger N of touch-points raises the future probability.

claims_checked: eclipse-related mentions per section: part1=55, part4=144, part5=20, part6=85, part8=13, part8b=39 (regex on stripped HTML, case-insensitive)
aetheric refraction sentence count: §1.5 cluster=21, §7.11 cluster=17 (regex on stripped HTML, restricted to sentences containing 'aetheric refraction' or 'n(r) =' substring)
OTS/timestamp mentions: part4=25, part6=36, part8=25, part8b=11 (4-section total = 97, plus part1=18, part5=12 = 127 total but core argument cluster is in the 4 named sections)
ensemble-anchor / W004 / magnitude-only: part2b=8, part4=8, part6=10, part8=3, part8b=18, part9=5 — total 52 across 6 sections
Sydney–Perth: 4 sections (part2=8, part5=20, part6=6, part7=5) matching c1 count of 4
tidal WIN detail_evidence lengths now differentiated (1209–2622 chars, unique content per constituent)
quantitative_verification.computations: Counts produced by python regex re.findall over the html field of each part, after stripping HTML tags with re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', html). Sentence-level analysis used re.split with sentence-boundary lookbehind then filtered by pattern presence. Spot-check verified by manual reading of the first 8 sentences in each cluster.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender reading this review could argue: 'Every major scientific review of a long-form theory restates its core findings across sections — IPCC AR6 mentions anthropogenic warming hundreds of times across thousands of pages because each chapter applies the finding to its domain. The dome-review document does the same: eclipse-Chapman appears in part1 (model description), part4 (live dashboard), part5 (kill shots), part6 (predictions analysis), part8 (AI directives), and part8b (author response) because each section views the finding through a domain-specific lens. The redundancy is structural and pedagogical, not bloat. A defender who cherry-picks two passages and claims contradiction is reading dishonestly — readers should expect the same finding to appear wherever it is analytically relevant.' This defense is rhetorically strong because the IPCC analogy is accurate and the layered-lens framing is exactly what part1's 'How This Review Is Organized' should communicate.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Two complementary fixes: (i) implement the Reading Guide paragraph (c1 stronger_arguments #1, still pending) — this directly explains why themes recur across tabs and pre-empts the 'padding' accusation; (ii) where re-derivation occurs, replace it with named cross-references rather than removing the argument entirely. The IPCC analogy holds for cross-chapter MENTION, NOT for re-derivation — the IPCC does not re-derive radiative forcing five times. The c2 consolidation recommendation maintains the IPCC-style cross-chapter coverage while eliminating the genuine re-derivation that a charitable critic would still call padding.

quantitative_summary.estimated_words_recoverable: 8,000–10,000 across all clusters if consolidated to single-source-with-references pattern (up from c1 estimate of 4,000–6,000, reflecting growth in document and addition of new clusters)
quantitative_summary.structural_risk: Cross-reference drift risk has GROWN since c1 because the document is larger and has more sections. The dome-author-contact development (CLAUDE.md April 2026) increases the consequence of inconsistent prose — a defender who finds one stale number in a re-derived block can use it to dismiss the whole review.

deeper_analysis:
Two structural observations distinguish c2 from c1.

First, the document grew substantially in the intervening 7 weeks (sections.json now 704 KB; part8b is a wholly new section ~30 KB; eclipse section 4.2 expanded from a short stub to 4.2.1–4.2.7a; new sections 6.10–6.14 added in part6; new section 8.17 added in part8). Growth came overwhelmingly from new analytical content, not from re-derivation. But each new content layer (the eclipse L1/L2/L3a/L3b decomposition, the OTS canary surfacing, the fair-auditor-list adjudication in part8b) was added to multiple sections rather than to one designated section with cross-references. The result is a document that is BOTH stronger analytically AND more redundant prosaically — a tension the consolidations above address.

Second, the dome-author-contact event (CLAUDE.md: 'the dome author has made direct contact with the operator via DM, Sunday 2026-04-26') changes the rhetorical stakes of consolidation. At c1, redundancy was a quality issue that affected only readers. At c2, redundancy interacts with the canary discipline: distributed redundancy means the dome author can patch the OTS methodology error without producing a clean canary fire — the operator-side detection needs a single canonical URL to compare against. Consolidating each major argument into a single canonical hub (with anchor-stable IDs) makes the canary detection sharper. This is a new argument for consolidation that did not exist at c1.

Priority for tightening (revised from c1): (1) Eclipse — now 7 sections, highest re-derivation count, the largest savings opportunity. (2) OTS/timestamp — NEW canary-discipline-relevant cluster. (3) Aetheric refraction — unchanged since c1, still 2 full treatments. (4) Ensemble-anchor / W004 — NEW analytical content not yet consolidated. (5) Relabeling — partially consolidated, low-hanging finish. (6) Sydney-Perth — mostly fine, small polish. (7) Reading Guide — independent stronger_argument that enables all the others.

One explicit non-recommendation: do NOT consolidate part8b's per-fair-auditor-point structure. That structure IS the adjudication frame and is uniquely useful. The recommendation is to keep the frame and remove the body re-derivations from the points themselves, replacing with cross-references to the canonical hubs (§4.2 for eclipse, §1.5 for refraction, §2b.6 for the magnitude-band arithmetic).

Finally: c1 estimated 4,000–6,000 recoverable words. c2 estimates 8,000–10,000. The increase reflects both document growth and new clusters. This is meaningful — the document is currently ~700 KB of HTML across 14 top-level parts; 8–10 KB of recoverable prose is roughly 1.5% of the document by raw size, but the recoverable portion is concentrated in the most-cited sections (part4, part6, part8b) where the redundancy is most visible to a critic.

citations_verified: Section 2.9 V13 coordinate system loop exists (verified)
Section 7.11 Aetheric Refraction: The Universal Escape Hatch exists (verified)
citations_failed: No section titled 'Timestamp Error' exists despite CLAUDE.md asserting one — this is a documentation-vs-deployment drift the integrity agent or tinker should reconcile (either build the section per CLAUDE.md or update CLAUDE.md to reflect §8.5 as the canonical home)

summary_for_decider: Holistic c2 review of HOL-REDUNDANCY (operator-commissioned Phase 2 rerun). recommended_action=minor_edit. 4 major holes (eclipse cluster worsened; aetheric refraction unchanged; NEW OTS/timestamp cluster; NEW ensemble-anchor cluster), 4 moderate (Sydney-Perth held; reading guide unimplemented; relabeling partially consolidated; 0 nT strawman scattered), 2 minor (tidal RESOLVED; zero-fitted-parameters/tracking minor unchanged). c1 stronger_arguments #1 (reading guide) is now MORE important — recommend the decider prioritize a small EXP to add the Reading Guide paragraph because it scaffolds all subsequent consolidations. Document-vs-CLAUDE.md drift flagged: CLAUDE.md asserts a 'Timestamp Error tab' that does not exist as a separate section. Counter-argument considered (IPCC-style cross-chapter mention is normal): defense_survives=3, mitigated by combining the reading guide + cross-references rather than removing content. No verdict change recommended; no batching authorized (holistic is singleton).
